info.name: RBAC – User Lock (/admin/users/{user_id}/lock)
info.description:
E2E tests for the admin user lock API.

Routes covered:
  POST   /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/lock   → 204 (lock user)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/lock   → 204 (unlock user)
  GET    /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/lock   → 200 (get lock status)

Folder execution order:
  1. setup            — register + Gmail OTP + verify-email + login (acting user)
                        register + verify-email + login (target user)
                        captures ul_access_token, ul_acting_user_id, ul_target_user_id
  2. auth-guard       — 401 on all three routes with no Authorization header
  3. rbac-guard       — 403 on all three routes with valid JWT but no user:lock / user:read permissions
  4. owner-bootstrap  — POST /owner/bootstrap promotes the acting user to owner
                        (grants user:lock + user:read; RBAC middleware reads DB per request)
  5. happy-path       — GET (target, not locked → 200 + shape) → POST /lock → 204 →
                        GET (target, locked → 200 + locked fields) → DELETE /lock → 204 →
                        GET (target, unlocked → 200 + admin_locked=false)
  6. conflict-guard   — 409 when attempting to lock own account (cannot_lock_self)
  7. not-found        — 404 on POST/DELETE/GET with unknown UUID; 404 on non-UUID user_id
  8. validation       — 422/413/400 input-error cases for POST /lock

Guard order (handler):
  POST   /users/{user_id}/lock   : AllowContentType → JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(user:lock) → handler
  DELETE /users/{user_id}/lock   : AllowContentType → JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(user:lock) → handler
  GET    /users/{user_id}/lock   : AllowContentType → JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(user:read) → handler

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in the running server's .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address for OTP receipt
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by unit/service/handler tests):
  - cannot_lock_owner (ErrCannotLockOwner → 409): requires a second owner in the DB;
    setup complexity is disproportionate for E2E; covered by handler_test.go + service_test.go.
  - DB / store errors → 500: handler_test.go, service_test.go.
  - actingUserID JWT sub is not a valid UUID → 500: service_test.go.
  - Login rejected when admin_locked=true: login handler_test.go + service_test.go.
  - context.WithoutCancel prevents client-disconnect from aborting lock/unlock write: store_test.go.
  - audit rows written (user_locked / user_unlocked events): store integration tests.

Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each suite run.
Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so admin-route requests never collide on any shared bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers two users:
  - Acting user (gmail_test_email): will be bootstrapped as owner and act as the caller for all lock operations.
  - Target user (gmail+ultarget variant): the account that will be locked/unlocked.

Both users are registered and email-verified. The acting user logs in and captures ul_access_token. The target user logs in so ul_target_user_id can be extracted from the JWT sub claim.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "ul_acting_code":', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] ul_acting_code is empty — Gmail fetch did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set and not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Captured ul_access_token and ul_acting_user_id:', payload.sub);
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch for target user'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for target user verification email...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API for target user OTP...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + targetEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found for target user.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts2.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts2.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode target email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in target email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "ul_target_code":', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] ul_target_code is empty — Gmail fetch for target user did not complete.');

item.name: POST /login (target user) → 200 (capture ul_target_user_id)
item.description: Target user is now verified. Logs in and captures ul_target_user_id from the JWT sub claim.

exec: console.warn('[WARN] Could not extract target user ID from JWT sub.');

item.name: auth-guard
item.description: Verifies that the JWTAuth middleware rejects requests with no Authorization header before RBAC or handler logic is reached. One test per HTTP method. POST carries Content-Type: application/json so the AllowContentType router middleware passes.

item.name: rbac-guard
item.description:
Verifies that RBAC.Require rejects a valid JWT belonging to a plain user who has not yet been bootstrapped. Must return 403 before any handler logic is called.

POST and DELETE require user:lock. GET requires user:read. Plain users have neither.

item.name: owner-bootstrap
item.description: Promotes the acting user to owner by calling POST /owner/bootstrap with bootstrap_secret. The owner role carries all permissions including user:lock and user:read. RBAC.Require reads the DB on every request — no re-login needed; the existing ul_access_token is sufficient for all subsequent folders.

item.name: POST /owner/bootstrap → 201 (grant owner role to acting user)

exec: console.error('[ERROR] ul_access_token not set — setup/POST /login did not complete.');
console.error('[ERROR] bootstrap_secret not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
console.log('[INFO] Acting user is now owner — RBAC.Require(user:lock) and RBAC.Require(user:read) will pass.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full lock/unlock lifecycle for the target user, authenticated as owner.

Execution order:
  1. GET  (target, not locked)    → 200; assert admin_locked=false, is_locked=false, no lock fields
  2. POST /lock                   → 204
  3. GET  (target, now locked)    → 200; assert admin_locked=true, locked_by=acting user, locked_reason set, locked_at set
  4. DELETE /lock                 → 204
  5. GET  (target, after unlock)  → 200; assert admin_locked=false, lock fields absent

exec: if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] ul_target_user_id not set — setup did not complete.'); }
pm.test('locked_by is absent when not locked', () => pm.expect(body.locked_by).to.be.undefined);
pm.test('locked_reason is absent when not locked', () => pm.expect(body.locked_reason).to.be.undefined);
pm.test('locked_at is absent when not locked', () => pm.expect(body.locked_at).to.be.undefined);
pm.test('login_locked_until is absent when not locked', () => pm.expect(body.login_locked_until).to.be.undefined);
if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] ul_target_user_id not set — setup did not complete.'); }
pm.test('admin_locked is true after POST /lock', () => pm.expect(body.admin_locked).to.be.true);
pm.test('locked_reason matches the reason sent in POST /lock', () => pm.expect(body.locked_reason).to.eql(expectedReason));
pm.test('locked_at is a parseable timestamp', () => pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(body.locked_at).getTime())).to.be.false);

item.name: GET /users/{target}/lock — after DELETE → 200 (admin_locked=false)
item.description: Confirms that UnlockUserTx cleared admin_locked and all lock metadata. All three lock fields must be absent from the response.

item.name: conflict-guard
item.description:
Verifies ErrCannotLockSelf (platformrbac.ErrCannotLockSelf → 409 cannot_lock_self).

The acting user is now the owner. Attempting to lock their own account fires guard #3 in LockUser (targetID == actorID) before any DB write.

Note: ErrCannotLockOwner (409 cannot_lock_owner) is NOT tested here because it requires a second owner account in the DB. That setup is disproportionate for E2E; it is fully covered by handler_test.go and service_test.go.

exec: if (!actingID) { console.error('[ERROR] ul_acting_user_id not set — setup/login did not complete.'); }

item.name: not-found
item.description:
Verifies 404 user_not_found responses when:
  - user_id is a valid UUID that does not exist in the DB (POST, DELETE, GET)
  - user_id is not a valid UUID string (POST, GET) — parseID fails → ErrUserNotFound

item.name: POST /users/{non_uuid}/lock — invalid UUID → 404 (parseID fails → ErrUserNotFound)
item.description: user_id is not a valid UUID string. Service.LockUser guard #1 (parseID) returns ErrUserNotFound → 404. Validation fires before any DB lookup.

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body decode and field validation cases for POST /users/{user_id}/lock.

validateLockUser checks:
  - reason must be non-empty after TrimSpace → ErrReasonRequired → 422 reason_required

All requests carry a valid owner JWT. Validation fires after RBAC.Require passes but before any service or DB call.

Note: DELETE and GET carry no body so they have no validation-layer cases beyond not-found.

item.name: POST /lock — whitespace-only reason → 422 reason_required
item.description: validateLockUser calls strings.TrimSpace — a reason of only spaces or tabs is treated as empty.

item.name: POST /lock — malformed JSON → 400 bad_request
item.description: respond.DecodeJSON rejects malformed bodies before validateLockUser is called.

item.name: POST /lock — body > 1 MiB → 413
item.description: http.MaxBytesReader in LockUser rejects oversized bodies before JSON decoding or validation.

exec: pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ reason: 'size test', _pad: pad }) });

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email. Used for acting user registration and login.
6-digit verification OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register (acting user). Consumed by setup/verify-email.
JWT access_token from setup/login (acting user). Plain user before owner-bootstrap; owner after. Reused for all subsequent folders without re-login.
User ID of the acting user extracted from the JWT sub claim. Used as the caller for lock operations and for the self-lock conflict test.
Derived email for the target user (gmail+ultarget variant). Set in setup prerequest.
6-digit verification OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register (target user). Consumed by setup/verify-email (target).
User ID of the target user. Captured from the target user's JWT sub claim. Used as the {user_id} path parameter for lock/unlock/status requests.
Lock reason sent in the happy-path POST /lock request. Asserted in the subsequent GET /lock response.
